hero.title: AI in 2026,
hero.subtitle: A practical guide for curious people who want to understand what's happening with AI — without the influencer noise. Real tools, real setups, honest takes.

models.title: Coding Models
models.subtitle: Benchmarks don't lie. Here's how the top models stack up on real coding tasks — not marketing slides.
models.benchmarkNote: SWE-bench Verified: real-world software engineering tasks. HumanEval: code generation accuracy.

agents.title: What are coding agents?
agents.subtitle: Forget autocomplete. Coding agents can read your files, run code, open your browser, push to GitHub, and iterate until the task is done. Here's how they compare.

whatIs.title: Agent vs Copilot
whatIs.copilot: A copilot suggests code as you type. You still do everything.
whatIs.agent: An agent takes a task and works autonomously: reads files, writes code, runs tests, fixes errors — on its own.

automation.title: What is workflow automation?
automation.subtitle: Instead of asking AI one question at a time, you build systems where agents work together — reading code, editing files, searching the web, and committing to GitHub without you touching anything.

l1.label: Level 1 — Awareness
l1.desc: You use AI tools like ChatGPT or Claude to get answers, generate text, summarize documents. You're in the loop for every step.

l2.label: Level 2 — Daily Usage
l2.desc: You use AI daily for coding help, prompting workflows, API integrations. You start chaining prompts together.

l3.label: Level 3 — Systems
l3.desc: AI doesn't just answer you — it works with you. Reads your files, opens your browser, pushes code. You build the system once, it runs.

setupItems: Claude Code reads and edits files in real time
Playwright opens and controls any browser
GitHub MCP pushes code, opens PRs, reviews issues
Swarm agents split tasks and coordinate in parallel
Sequential-thinking reasons step by step before acting

mcp.title: MCP: AI's plugin system
mcp.subtitle: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is how you give AI access to the real world — your files, your browser, your GitHub, your design tools. Pick your setup below.

skills.title: Skills for Claude Code
skills.subtitle: Skills are slash commands that give Claude Code specialized expertise. Type /senior-backend and Claude becomes a backend architect. Here are the ones actually worth installing.

howTo.title: How to install a skill
howTo.step2: Add your persona/instructions in the markdown file
howTo.step3: Use /your-skill in any Claude Code session

apis.title: Best free APIs to run powerful models
apis.subtitle: You don't need to pay to run serious AI models. These providers give you real rate limits, powerful models, and zero credit card required.
apis.highlight: Why Nvidia NIM is the current best-kept secret
apis.highlightDesc: Nvidia built a platform where every major AI model runs on their GPU infrastructure. Free tier gives you access to 100+ models with generous limits — specialized by use case. It's not well-known yet, but it's what professionals quietly use.

nvidiaModels.title: Nvidia NIM — Models by use case

sources.title: Where I get my info
sources.subtitle: You don't need to read everything. You need a system. Here's mine — the exact accounts, subreddits, and repos that surface the signal before the noise.

footer.tagline: A guide from someone building in this space — sharing what actually works.
